reason: Create persistent decision memory for agent workflow

decision: Adopt feature-oriented backend packages under main/features
reason: Prevent monolithic file growth and align code layout with PMO functional domains
impact: Improves maintainability, team parallelism, and roadmap scalability

decision: Keep legacy API endpoints while introducing /api/pmo/* scaffold routes
reason: Avoid frontend breakage during incremental migration
impact: Safe transition path with backward compatibility

decision: Mirror backend feature domains in frontend under features/pmo/modules/*
reason: Keep frontend and backend architecture isomorphic for maintainability and team scaling
impact: Reduces coupling and avoids oversized UI/API files

decision: Introduce authGet/authPost in api store as shared transport contract
reason: Standardize authenticated requests across all PMO feature modules
impact: Consistent API integration pattern and lower duplication

decision: Track roadmap as machine-readable task graph in repository
reason: Need deterministic source for auto-creating Bitrix24 tasks and dependency links
impact: Enables repeatable roadmap publication and status sync across chats

decision: Implement Bitrix24 sync CLI with dry-run default
reason: Prevent accidental writes and allow validation before applying to production project board
impact: Safer operations and easier onboarding for roadmap automation

decision: Use default RESPONSIBLE_ID=1 for roadmap publication
reason: Portal rejects task creation without assignee and explicit responsible IDs were not provided per-task
impact: Enabled successful creation of all roadmap tasks in Bitrix24 project

decision: Persist Bitrix task mapping in repository context
reason: Need deterministic key->task ID map for subsequent automated status updates

decision: Duplicate epic identifiers in Bitrix task tags
reason: Need fast filtering/grouping by epic in board views
impact: Improved operational visibility without changing task graph

decision: Execute only RD-001 and pause subsequent tasks
reason: User requested to review outcomes after first stage before continuing
impact: Controlled rollout and early feedback loop
